gazette: Directorate General of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Canada to Limit Steel Import Tariff Quotas to Half of 2024 Volume
bodyHtml:
On June 27, 2025, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced that tariff rate quotas (TRQs) would be imposed on steel imports from countries with which Canada does not have a free trade agreement. The TRQs will be based on 2024 import data, and any imports exceeding this level will be subject to a 50% duty. The related announcement can be found at the link below:

https://www.canada.ca/en/department-finance/news/2025/06/tariff-rate-quotas-on-imports-of-steel-mill-products.html

On July 16, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced new measures regarding steel imports.

Accordingly:

  • Canada will limit the tariff quota for countries without a free trade agreement to 50% of their 2024 import volumes. Imports from these countries exceeding 50% of their 2024 volume will be subject to a 50% customs duty.
  • For countries with a free trade agreement (excluding the United States), the steel import quota will be set at 100% of 2024 volumes. Imports exceeding these levels will be subject to a 50% duty.
  • A 25% duty will be imposed on imports containing steel that was melted and poured in China, regardless of the country of export (excluding the United States).
  • The federal government will prioritize the use of Canadian steel in large-scale national projects.

The official announcement by the Prime Minister's Office can be accessed via the link below:

https://www.pm.gc.ca/en/news/news-releases/2025/07/16/prime-minister-carney-announces-new-measures-protect-and-strength
